framework_source: DoD Cyber Workforce Framework Work Role Tool v5.1

use_case_per_ir8477_section3.target_audience: WIDAI framework developers, DoD workforce planners, PE operating partners evaluating organizations with federal/defense data and AI programs, and CDAIOs building workforce competency programs aligned with DoD requirements.
use_case_per_ir8477_section3.why_this_mapping: DCWF is the US federal government's authoritative cyber workforce framework with a dedicated Data/AI workforce element containing 11 work roles. It directly overlaps with WIDAI's scope — both frameworks define data, AI, and governance workforce competencies. This STRM validates WIDAI against the framework most likely to be cited in federal data/AI workforce requirements and identifies where DoD-specific competencies extend beyond WIDAI's current coverage.

concept_types: KSA statements (Knowledge, Skills, Abilities combined) — 1,668 elements
focal_document.exclusion_rationale: No elements excluded. All 2,945 KSAT elements evaluated. DCWF spans cybersecurity, IT, intelligence, cyber effects, enablers, software engineering, and Data/AI. Elements outside WIDAI scope produce 'No relationship' results documenting the boundary.

concept_types_mapped.structural_note: DCWF uses KSAT (Knowledge, Skills, Abilities, Tasks) building blocks derived from NICE Framework. The DCWF Work Role Tool v5.1 combines K, S, and A into a single 'KSA' type with Tasks separate. WIDAI uses KSA with distinct type labels. This structural difference is cosmetic — the mapping evaluates semantic content regardless of element type labels.

use_case_per_ir8477_section3.direction_of_mapping: Focal → Reference. Each DCWF KSAT element (focal) is evaluated against the WIDAI KSA pool (reference). A DCWF element with 'No relationship' to any WIDAI KSA indicates either (a) a tactical cybersecurity/intelligence competency outside WIDAI scope, or (b) a potential gap in WIDAI coverage.
use_case_per_ir8477_section3.exhaustiveness_level: Exhaustive. Every DCWF KSAT element (2,945) is evaluated. For each element, the most semantically relevant WIDAI KSA is identified via bi-encoder candidate identification, then scored through the full multi-method pipeline (identical to STRM-001-ONET and STRM-002-NICE).
use_case_per_ir8477_section3.rationale_type: Semantic (primary). DCWF and WIDAI share significant vocabulary overlap — both define data, AI, governance, and management competencies. Semantic rationale evaluates whether concepts describe the same or overlapping competencies.
use_case_per_ir8477_section3.rationale_justification: DCWF is a federal cyber workforce framework that includes a Data/AI workforce element. WIDAI is a data and AI workforce framework. The domain overlap is substantial — both frameworks define competencies for data officers, data architects, data stewards, AI specialists, and risk/ethics practitioners. Semantic rationale captures both the direct overlap and the boundary where DCWF extends into cybersecurity-specific territory.

scope_decisions.note: All 2,945 KSAT elements evaluated without categorical exclusions. The 10 'No relationship' results are analytically valuable — they identify the small number of purely tactical cybersecurity/intelligence elements with zero WIDAI relevance.

structural_elements_excluded.work_roles: 75 work roles are structural containers, not FDEs. Work roles group KSAT elements; the KSAT elements themselves are the mappable concepts.
structural_elements_excluded.workforce_elements: 7 workforce element categories are organizational groupings.

candidate_identification.description: Due to the volume of elements (2,945 × 497 = 1.46M potential pairs), bi-encoder embeddings (all-MiniLM-L6-v2) were used to identify the most semantically relevant WIDAI KSA candidate for each DCWF element. This is a preprocessing step for pairing, not a scoring method. Once pairs were established, every mapped pair was scored through the identical multi-method pipeline used in STRM-001-ONET and STRM-002-NICE.
candidate_identification.note: Candidate identification determines which WIDAI KSA each DCWF element is paired with. All scoring, classification, and significance generation is performed by the STRM scoring pipeline (strm/dcwf/strm_scoring_pipeline.py), methodologically identical to STRM-001 and STRM-002.
